  1037вҶ’## 3.8 Baseline & Drift Tables
  1038вҶ’
  1039вҶ’```sql
  1040вҶ’-- Plan baselines (immutable snapshots)
  1041вҶ’CREATE TABLE plan_baselines (
  1042вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1043вҶ’    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  1044вҶ’    description TEXT,
  1045вҶ’    version VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,  -- e.g., 'v3.2'
  1046вҶ’    
  1047вҶ’    baseline_date DATE NOT NULL,   -- "As of" date
  1048вҶ’    horizon_end DATE NOT NULL,     -- How far into future
  1049вҶ’    
  1050вҶ’    -- Workflow
  1051вҶ’    status baseline_status DEFAULT 'DRAFT',
  1052вҶ’    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  1053вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1054вҶ’    
  1055вҶ’    published_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  1056вҶ’    published_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  1057вҶ’    
  1058вҶ’    superseded_by UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
  1059вҶ’    superseded_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  1060вҶ’    
  1061вҶ’    -- Metrics at baseline time
  1062вҶ’    fleet_count INTEGER,
  1063вҶ’    total_pm_scheduled INTEGER,
  1064вҶ’    total_manhours_projected DECIMAL(12,2)
  1065вҶ’);
  1066вҶ’
  1067вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_baselines_status ON plan_baselines(status);
  1068вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_baselines_date ON plan_baselines(baseline_date DESC);
  1069вҶ’
  1070вҶ’-- Baseline PM schedule snapshot
  1071вҶ’CREATE TABLE baseline_pm_schedule (
  1072вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1073вҶ’    baseline_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_baselines(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  1074вҶ’    
  1075вҶ’    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
  1076вҶ’    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
  1077вҶ’    projected_date DATE NOT NULL,
  1078вҶ’    projected_mileage_km INTEGER,
  1079вҶ’    
  1080вҶ’    assigned_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
  1081вҶ’    assigned_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
  1082вҶ’    
  1083вҶ’    UNIQUE(baseline_id, train_id, pm_type_id, projected_date)
  1084вҶ’);
  1085вҶ’
  1086вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_baseline_pm_baseline ON baseline_pm_schedule(baseline_id);
  1087вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_baseline_pm_train ON baseline_pm_schedule(train_id, projected_date);
  1088вҶ’
  1089вҶ’-- Baseline mileage projections
  1090вҶ’CREATE TABLE baseline_mileage_projection (
  1091вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1092вҶ’    baseline_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_baselines(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  1093вҶ’    
  1094вҶ’    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
  1095вҶ’    projection_date DATE NOT NULL,
  1096вҶ’    projected_cumulative_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
  1097вҶ’    daily_rate_km INTEGER,  -- Assumed daily accrual rate
  1098вҶ’    
  1099вҶ’    UNIQUE(baseline_id, train_id, projection_date)
  1100вҶ’);
  1101вҶ’
  1102вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_baseline_mileage ON baseline_mileage_projection(baseline_id, train_id);
  1103вҶ’
  1104вҶ’-- Drift analysis results (generated by drift monitor)
  1105вҶ’CREATE TABLE drift_analysis (
  1106вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1107вҶ’    baseline_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
  1108вҶ’    analysis_date DATE NOT NULL,
  1109вҶ’    
  1110вҶ’    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
  1111вҶ’    
  1112вҶ’    -- Mileage drift
  1113вҶ’    baseline_projected_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
  1114вҶ’    actual_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
  1115вҶ’    mileage_drift_km INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS (actual_km - baseline_projected_km) STORED,
  1116вҶ’    mileage_drift_pct DECIMAL(5,2),
  1117вҶ’    
  1118вҶ’    -- Schedule drift (days)
  1119вҶ’    avg_pm_drift_days DECIMAL(5,1),
  1120вҶ’    max_pm_drift_days INTEGER,
  1121вҶ’    
  1122вҶ’    -- Threshold breach
  1123вҶ’    requires_replan BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  1124вҶ’    replan_reason TEXT,
  1125вҶ’    
  1126вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1127вҶ’    
  1128вҶ’    UNIQUE(baseline_id, train_id, analysis_date)
  1129вҶ’);
  1130вҶ’
  1131вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_drift_baseline ON drift_analysis(baseline_id, analysis_date);
  1132вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_drift_replan ON drift_analysis(requires_replan, analysis_date) 
  1133вҶ’    WHERE requires_replan = TRUE;
  1134вҶ’```

  1137вҶ’
  1138вҶ’## 3.9 Scenario Management Tables
  1139вҶ’
  1140вҶ’```sql
  1141вҶ’-- Scenarios (what-if analysis containers)
  1142вҶ’CREATE TABLE scenarios (
  1143вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1144вҶ’    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  1145вҶ’    description TEXT,
  1146вҶ’    
  1147вҶ’    -- Fork point
  1148вҶ’    forked_from_baseline_id UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
  1149вҶ’    forked_from_scenario_id UUID REFERENCES scenarios(id),
  1150вҶ’    forked_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1151вҶ’    
  1152вҶ’    -- Metadata
  1153вҶ’    status scenario_status DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
  1154вҶ’    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  1155вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1156вҶ’    
  1157вҶ’    -- Expiration (scenarios auto-expire after 30 days of inactivity)
  1158вҶ’    last_modified_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1159вҶ’    expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT (NOW() + INTERVAL '30 days'),
  1160вҶ’    
  1161вҶ’    -- Promotion
  1162вҶ’    promoted_to_baseline_id UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
  1163вҶ’    promoted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  1164вҶ’    promoted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
  1165вҶ’);
  1166вҶ’
  1167вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_scenarios_status ON scenarios(status);
  1168вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_scenarios_user ON scenarios(created_by, status);
  1169вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_scenarios_expiring ON scenarios(expires_at) WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';
  1170вҶ’
  1171вҶ’-- Scenario deltas (event-sourced changes)
  1172вҶ’CREATE TABLE scenario_deltas (
  1173вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1174вҶ’    scenario_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES scenarios(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  1175вҶ’    sequence_num INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Order of application
  1176вҶ’    
  1177вҶ’    delta_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  1178вҶ’    -- 'PM_MOVE', 'PM_ADD', 'PM_REMOVE', 'CAPACITY_CHANGE', 'TRAIN_STATUS_CHANGE'
  1179вҶ’    
  1180вҶ’    -- Reference to affected entity
  1181вҶ’    entity_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,  -- 'pm_schedule', 'manhour_capacity', 'trains'
  1182вҶ’    entity_id UUID NOT NULL,
  1183вҶ’    
  1184вҶ’    -- Change details (JSON)
  1185вҶ’    old_values JSONB,
  1186вҶ’    new_values JSONB,
  1187вҶ’    
  1188вҶ’    -- Explanation
  1189вҶ’    change_reason TEXT,
  1190вҶ’    
  1191вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1192вҶ’    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  1193вҶ’    
  1194вҶ’    UNIQUE(scenario_id, sequence_num)
  1195вҶ’);
  1196вҶ’
  1197вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_scenario_deltas_scenario ON scenario_deltas(scenario_id, sequence_num);
  1198вҶ’
  1199вҶ’-- Scenario comparison results (materialized for performance)
  1200вҶ’CREATE TABLE scenario_comparisons (
  1201вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1202вҶ’    scenario_a_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES scenarios(id),
  1203вҶ’    scenario_b_id UUID REFERENCES scenarios(id),  -- NULL = compare to baseline
  1204вҶ’    baseline_id UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
  1205вҶ’    
  1206вҶ’    comparison_date DATE NOT NULL,
  1207вҶ’    
  1208вҶ’    -- Summary metrics
  1209вҶ’    pm_count_delta INTEGER,
  1210вҶ’    total_hours_delta DECIMAL(10,2),
  1211вҶ’    total_cost_delta DECIMAL(12,2),
  1212вҶ’    overloaded_weeks_delta INTEGER,
  1213вҶ’    
  1214вҶ’    -- Detailed differences (JSON)
  1215вҶ’    pm_differences JSONB,
  1216вҶ’    manhour_differences JSONB,
  1217вҶ’    
  1218вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
  1219вҶ’);
  1220вҶ’```

  1224вҶ’## 3.10 Costing Tables
  1225вҶ’
  1226вҶ’```sql
  1227вҶ’-- Labor rates
  1228вҶ’CREATE TABLE labor_rates (
  1229вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1230вҶ’    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),  -- NULL = global default
  1231вҶ’    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
  1232вҶ’    effective_to DATE,
  1233вҶ’    
  1234вҶ’    -- Rate types
  1235вҶ’    regular_hourly_rate DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
  1236вҶ’    overtime_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 1.5,
  1237вҶ’    weekend_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 2.0,
  1238вҶ’    holiday_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 2.5,
  1239вҶ’    
  1240вҶ’    -- Shop rate (fully loaded)
  1241вҶ’    shop_rate_hourly DECIMAL(10,2),  -- Includes overhead, benefits
  1242вҶ’    
  1243вҶ’    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
  1244вҶ’    
  1245вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1246вҶ’    
  1247вҶ’    UNIQUE(depot_id, effective_from)
  1248вҶ’);
  1249вҶ’
  1250вҶ’-- Material cost history
  1251вҶ’CREATE TABLE material_costs (
  1252вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1253вҶ’    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  1254вҶ’    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
  1255вҶ’    effective_to DATE,
  1256вҶ’    
  1257вҶ’    unit_cost DECIMAL(12,2) NOT NULL,
  1258вҶ’    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
  1259вҶ’    supplier VARCHAR(100),
  1260вҶ’    
  1261вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1262вҶ’    
  1263вҶ’    UNIQUE(part_number, effective_from)
  1264вҶ’);
  1265вҶ’
  1266вҶ’-- Cost baselines (for variance analysis)
  1267вҶ’CREATE TABLE cost_baselines (
  1268вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1269вҶ’    fiscal_year INTEGER NOT NULL,
  1270вҶ’    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
  1271вҶ’    
  1272вҶ’    budget_labor_hours DECIMAL(12,2),
  1273вҶ’    budget_labor_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
  1274вҶ’    budget_material_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
  1275вҶ’    budget_total DECIMAL(14,2),
  1276вҶ’    
  1277вҶ’    -- Actuals (updated monthly)
  1278вҶ’    actual_labor_hours DECIMAL(12,2),
  1279вҶ’    actual_labor_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
  1280вҶ’    actual_material_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
  1281вҶ’    actual_total DECIMAL(14,2),
  1282вҶ’    
  1283вҶ’    variance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
  1284вҶ’        (CASE WHEN budget_total > 0 
  1285вҶ’              THEN ((actual_total - budget_total) / budget_total * 100)
  1286вҶ’              ELSE 0 END) STORED,
  1287вҶ’    
  1288вҶ’    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1289вҶ’    
  1290вҶ’    UNIQUE(fiscal_year, depot_id)
  1291вҶ’);
  1292вҶ’```

  1429вҶ’
  1430вҶ’## 3.13 Canvas-Optimized Views
  1431вҶ’
  1432вҶ’```sql
  1433вҶ’-- Strategic zoom level (yearly aggregates)
  1434вҶ’CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_strategic AS
  1435вҶ’SELECT 
  1436вҶ’    t.id as train_id,
  1437вҶ’    t.train_number,
  1438вҶ’    t.fleet_id,
  1439вҶ’    date_trunc('year', ps.target_date) as year,
  1440вҶ’    COUNT(*) as pm_count,
  1441вҶ’    SUM(pt.estimated_hours) as total_hours,
  1442вҶ’    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ps.status = 'COMPLETED') as completed_count,
  1443вҶ’    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ps.is_locked) as locked_count,
  1444вҶ’    SUM(pt.estimated_hours) FILTER (WHERE ps.status = 'SCHEDULED') as pending_hours
  1445вҶ’FROM trains t
  1446вҶ’LEFT JOIN pm_schedule ps ON t.id = ps.train_id AND ps.scenario_id IS NULL
  1447вҶ’LEFT JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
  1448вҶ’WHERE t.active = TRUE
  1449вҶ’GROUP BY t.id, t.train_number, t.fleet_id, date_trunc('year', ps.target_date);
  1450вҶ’
  1451вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_strategic_train ON v_canvas_strategic(train_id, year);
  1452вҶ’
  1453вҶ’-- Tactical zoom level (weekly aggregates with PM details)
  1454вҶ’CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_tactical AS
  1455вҶ’SELECT 
  1456вҶ’    t.id as train_id,
  1457вҶ’    t.train_number,
  1458вҶ’    date_trunc('week', ps.target_date) as week_start,
  1459вҶ’    ps.id as pm_id,
  1460вҶ’    ps.target_date,
  1461вҶ’    ps.status,
  1462вҶ’    ps.constraint_start,
  1463вҶ’    ps.constraint_end,
  1464вҶ’    ps.is_locked,
  1465вҶ’    pt.pm_code,
  1466вҶ’    pt.estimated_hours,
  1467вҶ’    pt.display_color,
  1468вҶ’    tr.track_number,
  1469вҶ’    d.code as depot_code
  1470вҶ’FROM trains t
  1471вҶ’JOIN pm_schedule ps ON t.id = ps.train_id AND ps.scenario_id IS NULL
  1472вҶ’JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
  1473вҶ’LEFT JOIN tracks tr ON ps.assigned_track_id = tr.id
  1474вҶ’LEFT JOIN depots d ON ps.assigned_depot_id = d.id
  1475вҶ’WHERE t.active = TRUE
  1476вҶ’  AND ps.status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS');
  1477вҶ’
  1478вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_tactical_train ON v_canvas_tactical(train_id, week_start);
  1479вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_tactical_date ON v_canvas_tactical(target_date);
  1480вҶ’
  1481вҶ’-- Man-hour canvas overlay
  1482вҶ’CREATE VIEW v_manhour_canvas AS
  1483вҶ’SELECT 
  1484вҶ’    mp.depot_id,
  1485вҶ’    d.code as depot_code,
  1486вҶ’    mp.period_start,
  1487вҶ’    mp.period_end,
  1488вҶ’    mp.required_total_hours,
  1489вҶ’    mp.available_total_hours,
  1490вҶ’    mp.variance_hours,
  1491вҶ’    mp.utilization_pct,
  1492вҶ’    mp.is_overloaded,
  1493вҶ’    mp.is_underutilized,
  1494вҶ’    COALESCE(adj.adjustment_count, 0) as pending_adjustments,
  1495вҶ’    COALESCE(adj.total_adjustment_hours, 0) as proposed_adjustment_hours
  1496вҶ’FROM manhour_projections mp
  1497вҶ’JOIN depots d ON mp.depot_id = d.id
  1498вҶ’LEFT JOIN (
  1499вҶ’    SELECT 
  1500вҶ’        projection_id,
  1501вҶ’        COUNT(*) as adjustment_count,
  1502вҶ’        SUM(hours_adjusted) as total_adjustment_hours
  1503вҶ’    FROM manhour_adjustments
  1504вҶ’    WHERE status = 'PROPOSED'
  1505вҶ’    GROUP BY projection_id
  1506вҶ’) adj ON mp.id = adj.projection_id
  1507вҶ’WHERE mp.projection_run_id = (
  1508вҶ’    SELECT projection_run_id 
  1509вҶ’    FROM manhour_projections 
  1510вҶ’    ORDER BY created_at DESC 
  1511вҶ’    LIMIT 1
  1512вҶ’);
  1513вҶ’
  1514вҶ’-- Drift overlay (comparison to baseline)
  1515вҶ’CREATE VIEW v_drift_canvas AS
  1516вҶ’SELECT 
  1517вҶ’    da.train_id,
  1518вҶ’    t.train_number,
  1519вҶ’    da.baseline_id,
  1520вҶ’    pb.name as baseline_name,
  1521вҶ’    da.analysis_date,
  1522вҶ’    da.mileage_drift_km,
  1523вҶ’    da.mileage_drift_pct,
  1524вҶ’    da.avg_pm_drift_days,
  1525вҶ’    da.requires_replan,
  1526вҶ’    da.replan_reason,
  1527вҶ’    -- Color coding for canvas
  1528вҶ’    CASE 
  1529вҶ’        WHEN da.requires_replan THEN 'RED'
  1530вҶ’        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 3 THEN 'YELLOW'
  1531вҶ’        ELSE 'GREEN'
  1532вҶ’    END as drift_severity
  1533вҶ’FROM drift_analysis da
  1534вҶ’JOIN trains t ON da.train_id = t.id
  1535вҶ’JOIN plan_baselines pb ON da.baseline_id = pb.id
  1536вҶ’WHERE pb.status = 'PUBLISHED'
  1537вҶ’  AND da.analysis_date = (
  1538вҶ’      SELECT MAX(analysis_date) 
  1539вҶ’      FROM drift_analysis 
  1540вҶ’      WHERE baseline_id = da.baseline_id
  1541вҶ’  );
  1542вҶ’
  1543вҶ’-- Refresh materialized views (scheduled job)
  1544вҶ’-- REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
  1545вҶ’-- REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
  1546вҶ’```
  1547вҶ’

  1549вҶ’
  1550вҶ’## 3.14 Database Maintenance
  1551вҶ’
  1552вҶ’```sql
  1553вҶ’-- Automatic refresh of materialized views
  1554вҶ’CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION refresh_canvas_views()
  1555вҶ’RETURNS void AS $$
  1556вҶ’BEGIN
  1557вҶ’    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
  1558вҶ’    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
  1559вҶ’END;
  1560вҶ’$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1561вҶ’
  1562вҶ’-- Schedule refresh every 15 minutes (use pg_cron or external scheduler)
  1563вҶ’-- SELECT cron.schedule('*/15 * * * *', 'SELECT refresh_canvas_views()');
  1564вҶ’
  1565вҶ’-- Cleanup expired scenarios
  1566вҶ’CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION cleanup_expired_scenarios()
  1567вҶ’RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
  1568вҶ’DECLARE
  1569вҶ’    deleted_count INTEGER;
  1570вҶ’BEGIN
  1571вҶ’    UPDATE scenarios 
  1572вҶ’    SET status = 'EXPIRED'
  1573вҶ’    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' 
  1574вҶ’      AND expires_at < NOW();
  1575вҶ’    
  1576вҶ’    GET DIAGNOSTICS deleted_count = ROW_COUNT;
  1577вҶ’    RETURN deleted_count;
  1578вҶ’END;
  1579вҶ’$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1580вҶ’
  1581вҶ’-- Release expired soft reservations
  1582вҶ’CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION release_expired_reservations()
  1583вҶ’RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
  1584вҶ’DECLARE
  1585вҶ’    released_count INTEGER;
  1586вҶ’BEGIN
  1587вҶ’    -- Update inventory positions
  1588вҶ’    UPDATE inventory_positions ip
  1589вҶ’    SET quantity_reserved = quantity_reserved - expired.total_qty
  1590вҶ’    FROM (
  1591вҶ’        SELECT inventory_position_id, SUM(quantity_reserved) as total_qty
  1592вҶ’        FROM material_reservations
  1593вҶ’        WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
  1594вҶ’          AND expires_at < NOW()
  1595вҶ’          AND fulfilled_at IS NULL
  1596вҶ’        GROUP BY inventory_position_id
  1597вҶ’    ) expired
  1598вҶ’    WHERE ip.id = expired.inventory_position_id;
  1599вҶ’    
  1600вҶ’    -- Delete expired reservations
  1601вҶ’    DELETE FROM material_reservations
  1602вҶ’    WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
  1603вҶ’      AND expires_at < NOW()
  1604вҶ’      AND fulfilled_at IS NULL;
  1605вҶ’    
  1606вҶ’    GET DIAGNOSTICS released_count = ROW_COUNT;
  1607вҶ’    RETURN released_count;
  1608вҶ’END;
  1609вҶ’$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1610вҶ’
  1611вҶ’-- Update train target mileage for fleet balancing
  1612вҶ’CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_fleet_mileage_targets()
  1613вҶ’RETURNS void AS $$
  1614вҶ’BEGIN
  1615вҶ’    UPDATE trains t
  1616вҶ’    SET target_mileage_km = fleet_avg.avg_mileage
  1617вҶ’    FROM (
  1618вҶ’        SELECT fleet_id, AVG(current_mileage_km)::INTEGER as avg_mileage
  1619вҶ’        FROM trains
  1620вҶ’        WHERE active = TRUE
  1621вҶ’        GROUP BY fleet_id
  1622вҶ’    ) fleet_avg
  1623вҶ’    WHERE t.fleet_id = fleet_avg.fleet_id
  1624вҶ’      AND t.active = TRUE;
  1625вҶ’END;
  1626вҶ’$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1627вҶ’```
  1628вҶ’

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 3: Database Schema

**Document Version:** 2.1  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT  
**Last Updated:** 2026-01-19

---

## 3.1 Schema Overview

The database schema is organized into the following domains:

| Domain | Tables | Purpose |
|--------|--------|---------|
| Core Fleet | `fleets`, `trains`, `depots`, `routes` | Asset master data |
| **Operational Constraints** | `service_demand`, `route_utilization_profiles`, `train_route_compatibility` | **Operational requirements and constraints** |
| **Unavailability** | `unavailability_events` | **Corrective maintenance and incident tracking** |
| Mileage Tracking | `mileage_readings`, `meter_events` | Time-series odometer data |
| Component Tracking | `train_components`, `component_mileage` | Component-level lifecycle |
| PM Management | `pm_types`, `pm_schedule`, `pm_material_requirements` | Maintenance planning |
| **Track Capabilities** | `track_capabilities`, `pm_capability_requirements` | **Qualified resource matching** |
| **Data Migration** | `pm_baseline_assumptions` | **Cold start and history inference** |
| Material Management | `inventory_positions`, `material_reservations` | Parts availability |
| Man-hour Planning | `manhour_capacity`, `manhour_projections`, `manhour_adjustments` | Workforce balancing |
| Baseline & Drift | `plan_baselines`, `baseline_pm_schedule`, `baseline_mileage_projection` | Plan stability |
| Scenario Management | `scenarios`, `scenario_deltas` | What-if analysis |
| Costing | `labor_rates`, `material_costs`, `cost_baselines` | Financial tracking |
| Audit & History | `pm_schedule_history`, `audit_log` | Change tracking |
| Users & Auth | `users`, `roles`, `sessions` | Access control |

> **v2.1 Changes:** Added operational constraints domain (service demand, route utilization, train-route compatibility), unavailability events, track capabilities with PM requirements, and data migration assumptions tables.

---

## 3.2 Core Fleet Tables

```sql
-- Enable required extensions
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS timescaledb;

-- Enumerated types
CREATE TYPE fitness_status AS ENUM ('FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED');
CREATE TYPE pm_status AS ENUM ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS', 'COMPLETED', 'CANCELLED', 'DEFERRED');
CREATE TYPE track_type AS ENUM ('LIGHT', 'HEAVY', 'OVERHAUL', 'STABLING');
CREATE TYPE shift_type AS ENUM ('DAY', 'NIGHT', 'WEEKEND', 'HOLIDAY');
CREATE TYPE adjustment_status AS ENUM ('PROPOSED', 'APPROVED', 'REJECTED', 'APPLIED');
CREATE TYPE baseline_status AS ENUM ('DRAFT', 'PUBLISHED', 'SUPERSEDED', 'ARCHIVED');
CREATE TYPE scenario_status AS ENUM ('ACTIVE', 'PROMOTED', 'DISCARDED', 'EXPIRED');
CREATE TYPE material_criticality AS ENUM ('BLOCKING', 'DEFERRABLE', 'CONSUMABLE');
CREATE TYPE reservation_type AS ENUM ('SOFT', 'HARD');

-- NEW: v2.1 enum types
CREATE TYPE day_type AS ENUM ('WEEKDAY', 'SATURDAY', 'SUNDAY', 'HOLIDAY');
CREATE TYPE unavailability_type AS ENUM ('CORRECTIVE', 'INCIDENT', 'COLLISION', 'VANDALISM', 'EXTERNAL', 'PLANNED_OUTAGE');
CREATE TYPE unavailability_status AS ENUM ('ACTIVE', 'RESOLVED');
CREATE TYPE track_capability AS ENUM ('PIT', 'CRANE', 'LIFTING_JACKS', 'WHEEL_LATHE', 'CATENARY_ISOLATION', 'ROOF_ACCESS', 'WASH', 'DROP_TABLE');
CREATE TYPE constraint_type AS ENUM ('SIGNALING', 'GAUGE', 'POWER_SUPPLY', 'PLATFORM_LENGTH', 'MAX_SPEED', 'TUNNEL_CLEARANCE');
CREATE TYPE assumption_type AS ENUM ('INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING', 'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM', 'MANUAL_OVERRIDE', 'IMPORTED', 'NO_HISTORY');
CREATE TYPE confidence_level AS ENUM ('HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW', 'ESTIMATED');

-- Depots
CREATE TABLE depots (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    code VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    location VARCHAR(200),
    timezone VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'Asia/Singapore',
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Fleets (train types/manufacturers)
CREATE TABLE fleets (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    code VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL UNIQUE,  -- 'KS', 'ALSTOM'
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    manufacturer VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    model VARCHAR(100),
    base_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    cars_per_train INTEGER DEFAULT 6,
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Trains
CREATE TABLE trains (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
    train_number VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    commissioning_date DATE,
    current_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    fitness_status fitness_status DEFAULT 'FIT',
    fitness_notes TEXT,
    current_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    home_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    
    -- For mileage balancing
    target_mileage_km INTEGER,  -- Calculated target for fleet balance
    mileage_variance_km INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (current_mileage_km - COALESCE(target_mileage_km, current_mileage_km)) STORED,
    
    -- Canvas display properties
    display_color VARCHAR(7),
    display_order INTEGER,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fleet ON trains(fleet_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fitness ON trains(fitness_status) WHERE active = TRUE;
CREATE INDEX idx_trains_depot ON trains(current_depot_id);

-- Routes
CREATE TABLE routes (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    route_code VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    route_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    line_id VARCHAR(20),
    expected_km_per_day INTEGER NOT NULL,
    required_trains INTEGER NOT NULL,
    
    -- NEW v2.1: Route characteristics for compatibility matching
    default_daily_km INTEGER,                        -- Default mileage for projection
    signaling_system VARCHAR(30),                    -- 'ATC', 'CBTC', 'ERTMS', 'LEGACY'
    power_system VARCHAR(30),                        -- 'THIRD_RAIL_750V', 'OVERHEAD_25KV', 'OVERHEAD_1500V'
    tunnel_clearance_mm INTEGER,                     -- Maximum vehicle width allowed
    platform_length_m DECIMAL(5,2),                  -- Platform length constraint
    max_axle_load_tonnes DECIMAL(4,2),               -- Track weight limit
    
    -- Restrictions
    allowed_fleet_ids UUID[],
    requires_ato BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    min_cars INTEGER,
    max_cars INTEGER,
    
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Tracks (maintenance bays)
CREATE TABLE tracks (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
    track_number VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    track_type track_type NOT NULL,
    max_train_length_m DECIMAL(5,2),
    has_overhead_crane BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    has_pit_access BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(depot_id, track_number)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_tracks_depot_type ON tracks(depot_id, track_type) WHERE active = TRUE;
```

---

## 3.2.1 Service Demand Tables (NEW v2.1)

Service demand defines the minimum number of trains required for passenger operations by day type. This is a **hard constraint** for the optimizer - it cannot schedule maintenance that would violate availability targets.

```sql
-- Service demand by fleet and day type
CREATE TABLE service_demand (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
    effective_date DATE NOT NULL,
    day_type day_type NOT NULL,  -- 'WEEKDAY', 'SATURDAY', 'SUNDAY', 'HOLIDAY'
    
    -- Minimum availability requirements
    min_trains_required INTEGER NOT NULL,
    peak_am_requirement INTEGER,       -- 6:00-9:00 requirement
    midday_requirement INTEGER,        -- 9:00-16:00 requirement  
    peak_pm_requirement INTEGER,       -- 16:00-19:00 requirement
    evening_requirement INTEGER,       -- 19:00-23:00 requirement
    standby_buffer INTEGER DEFAULT 2,  -- Additional trains for contingency
    
    -- Metadata
    source VARCHAR(50),                -- 'OPERATIONS', 'CONTRACT', 'MANUAL'
    notes TEXT,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    
    UNIQUE(fleet_id, effective_date, day_type)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_service_demand_fleet ON service_demand(fleet_id, effective_date DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_service_demand_lookup ON service_demand(fleet_id, day_type, effective_date DESC);

-- Example data:
-- INSERT INTO service_demand (fleet_id, effective_date, day_type, min_trains_required, peak_am_requirement, standby_buffer)
-- VALUES ('fleet-ks-uuid', '2026-01-01', 'WEEKDAY', 45, 52, 3);
-- VALUES ('fleet-ks-uuid', '2026-01-01', 'SATURDAY', 32, 35, 2);
-- VALUES ('fleet-ks-uuid', '2026-01-01', 'SUNDAY', 28, 30, 2);
```

---

## 3.2.2 Route Utilization Profiles (NEW v2.1)

Route utilization profiles capture the expected daily mileage by route, enabling accurate long-term projections. Without this, mileage projection uses fleet-wide averages which can be off by 60,000+ km over 12 years.

```sql
-- Route-specific utilization profiles
CREATE TABLE route_utilization_profiles (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    route_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES routes(id),
    effective_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    
    -- Daily mileage by day type (km)
    weekday_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    saturday_km INTEGER,
    sunday_km INTEGER,
    holiday_km INTEGER,
    
    -- Seasonal adjustments
    peak_season_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 1.0,  -- e.g., 1.15 for CNY
    off_peak_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 1.0,
    
    -- Service characteristics
    service_hours_per_day DECIMAL(4,1),               -- Operating hours
    round_trip_km DECIMAL(6,2),                       -- Single round trip distance
    trips_per_day INTEGER,                            -- Expected service runs
    
    -- Metadata
    source VARCHAR(50),                               -- 'MEASURED', 'ESTIMATED', 'CONTRACT'
    confidence confidence_level DEFAULT 'MEDIUM',
    notes TEXT,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(route_id, effective_date)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_route_util_route ON route_utilization_profiles(route_id, effective_date DESC);

-- Example data:
-- North-South Line: High utilization (350 km/day weekday)
-- INSERT INTO route_utilization_profiles (route_id, effective_date, weekday_km, saturday_km, sunday_km, confidence)
-- VALUES ('route-nsl-uuid', '2026-01-01', 350, 280, 240, 'HIGH');
-- East-West Line: Medium utilization (220 km/day weekday)
-- VALUES ('route-ewl-uuid', '2026-01-01', 220, 180, 150, 'HIGH');
```

---

## 3.2.3 Unavailability Events (NEW v2.1)

Unavailability events track trains that cannot be scheduled due to corrective maintenance, incidents, or other blocking factors. This is a **critical integration point** with Maximo CMMS.

```sql
-- Unavailability events (corrective maintenance, incidents, etc.)
CREATE TABLE unavailability_events (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    
    -- Event classification
    event_type unavailability_type NOT NULL,  -- 'CORRECTIVE', 'INCIDENT', etc.
    status unavailability_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
    restriction_level fitness_status NOT NULL, -- Uses existing ENUM: 'RESTRICTED' or 'GROUNDED'
    
    -- Timing
    reported_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    estimated_repair_hours INTEGER,
    estimated_return_date DATE,
    actual_return_date DATE,
    
    -- External system linkage
    source_system VARCHAR(30),        -- 'MAXIMO', 'OCC', 'MANUAL'
    external_reference VARCHAR(50),   -- Maximo WO number (e.g., 'WO-2026-001234')
    external_status VARCHAR(30),      -- Maximo status (e.g., 'INPRG', 'WAPPR')
    
    -- Description
    description TEXT,
    failure_code VARCHAR(30),         -- Standard failure classification
    affected_system VARCHAR(50),      -- 'TRACTION', 'BRAKES', 'DOORS', 'HVAC', etc.
    
    -- Scheduling impact flags
    blocks_pm_scheduling BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    blocks_run_assignment BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    
    -- Resolution
    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    resolved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    resolution_notes TEXT,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    -- Constraint: Cannot have resolved data if status is ACTIVE
    CONSTRAINT chk_resolved_status CHECK (
        (status = 'ACTIVE' AND resolved_at IS NULL) OR
        (status = 'RESOLVED' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL)
    )
);

-- Primary query: Find active unavailability for a train
CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_active ON unavailability_events(train_id, status) 
    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';
-- Dashboard query: All active unavailability by estimated return
CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_return ON unavailability_events(estimated_return_date) 
    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';
-- Maximo sync: Find by external reference
CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_external ON unavailability_events(source_system, external_reference);
-- Audit: Find by type and date range
CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_audit ON unavailability_events(event_type, reported_at DESC);

-- Trigger to update train fitness_status when unavailability changes
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    IF TG_OP = 'INSERT' OR TG_OP = 'UPDATE' THEN
        IF NEW.status = 'ACTIVE' THEN
            UPDATE trains 
            SET fitness_status = NEW.restriction_level,
                fitness_notes = COALESCE(NEW.description, 'Unavailability event: ' || NEW.event_type),
                updated_at = NOW()
            WHERE id = NEW.train_id;
        ELSIF NEW.status = 'RESOLVED' THEN
            -- Only reset to FIT if no other active unavailability exists
            IF NOT EXISTS (
                SELECT 1 FROM unavailability_events 
                WHERE train_id = NEW.train_id 
                AND status = 'ACTIVE' 
                AND id != NEW.id
            ) THEN
                UPDATE trains 
                SET fitness_status = 'FIT',
                    fitness_notes = NULL,
                    updated_at = NOW()
                WHERE id = NEW.train_id;
            END IF;
        END IF;
    END IF;
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER trg_sync_train_fitness
AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE ON unavailability_events
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability();
```

---

## 3.2.4 Train-Route Compatibility (NEW v2.1)

Defines which trains can operate on which routes based on technical constraints (signaling, gauge, power supply, etc.). Prevents the optimizer from assigning trains to incompatible routes.

```sql
-- Train-route compatibility matrix
CREATE TABLE train_route_compatibility (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    
    -- Can be at train or fleet level
    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
    fleet_id UUID REFERENCES fleets(id),
    route_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES routes(id),
    
    -- Compatibility determination
    is_compatible BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    constraint_type constraint_type,   -- 'SIGNALING', 'GAUGE', 'POWER_SUPPLY', etc.
    restriction_reason TEXT,           -- Human-readable explanation
    
    -- Validity period (for temporary restrictions)
    effective_date DATE DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    expiry_date DATE,                  -- NULL = permanent
    
    -- Metadata
    verified_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    verified_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    notes TEXT,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    -- Must specify either train_id OR fleet_id, not both
    CONSTRAINT chk_train_or_fleet CHECK (
        (train_id IS NOT NULL AND fleet_id IS NULL) OR
        (train_id IS NULL AND fleet_id IS NOT NULL)
    )
);

CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_route ON train_route_compatibility(route_id, is_compatible);
CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_train ON train_route_compatibility(train_id, is_compatible) 
    WHERE train_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_fleet ON train_route_compatibility(fleet_id, is_compatible) 
    WHERE fleet_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_active ON train_route_compatibility(effective_date, expiry_date)
    WHERE is_compatible = FALSE;

-- Example data:
-- Fleet KS cannot run on Line 4 (no CBTC equipment)
-- INSERT INTO train_route_compatibility (fleet_id, route_id, is_compatible, constraint_type, restriction_reason)
-- VALUES ('fleet-ks-uuid', 'route-line4-uuid', FALSE, 'SIGNALING', 'KS fleet lacks CBTC - only ATC equipped');

-- Train 1042 temporarily restricted from NEL (oversized pantograph)
-- INSERT INTO train_route_compatibility (train_id, route_id, is_compatible, constraint_type, restriction_reason, effective_date, expiry_date)
-- VALUES ('train-1042-uuid', 'route-nel-uuid', FALSE, 'TUNNEL_CLEARANCE', 'Temporary - pantograph modification pending', '2026-01-01', '2026-03-31');
```

---

## 3.2.5 Track Capabilities (NEW v2.1)

Defines the specific equipment and features available on each maintenance track. Enables constraint-based scheduling that matches PM requirements to track capabilities.

```sql
-- Track capability definitions
CREATE TABLE track_capabilities (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    track_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tracks(id),
    capability track_capability NOT NULL,
    -- 'PIT', 'CRANE', 'LIFTING_JACKS', 'WHEEL_LATHE', 
    -- 'CATENARY_ISOLATION', 'ROOF_ACCESS', 'WASH', 'DROP_TABLE'
    
    -- Capacity limits for this capability
    capacity_limit JSONB,  -- e.g., {"max_axle_load_tonnes": 17, "max_lift_tonnes": 25}
    
    -- Availability
    is_operational BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    maintenance_schedule TEXT,         -- e.g., "Unavailable Tuesdays 06:00-10:00"
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(track_id, capability)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_track_cap_track ON track_capabilities(track_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_track_cap_type ON track_capabilities(capability) WHERE is_operational = TRUE;

-- PM type capability requirements
CREATE TABLE pm_capability_requirements (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
    capability track_capability NOT NULL,
    is_mandatory BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,  -- FALSE = preferred but not required
    
    notes TEXT,                         -- Explanation of why this capability is needed
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(pm_type_id, capability)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_pm_cap_req_pm ON pm_capability_requirements(pm_type_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_cap_req_cap ON pm_capability_requirements(capability);

-- Example data:
-- Track T1 at Bishan has pit, crane, and lifting jacks
-- INSERT INTO track_capabilities (track_id, capability)
-- VALUES ('track-t1-bishan-uuid', 'PIT'),
--        ('track-t1-bishan-uuid', 'CRANE'),
--        ('track-t1-bishan-uuid', 'LIFTING_JACKS');

-- C4 overhaul requires pit and lifting jacks (mandatory), crane (preferred)
-- INSERT INTO pm_capability_requirements (pm_type_id, capability, is_mandatory)
-- VALUES ('pm-c4-uuid', 'PIT', TRUE),
--        ('pm-c4-uuid', 'LIFTING_JACKS', TRUE),
--        ('pm-c4-uuid', 'CRANE', FALSE);
```

---

## 3.2.6 PM Baseline Assumptions (NEW v2.1)

Handles cold start scenarios where PM history is incomplete during data migration. Tracks assumptions with confidence levels and flags records requiring manual review.

```sql
-- PM history baseline assumptions for cold start scenarios
CREATE TABLE pm_baseline_assumptions (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
    
    -- Assumption details
    assumption_type assumption_type NOT NULL,
    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING' - assume first PM at commissioning date + interval
    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM' - derived from C4/OH completion implies C1/C2/C3 done
    -- 'MANUAL_OVERRIDE' - operator entered value
    -- 'IMPORTED' - from legacy system with known quality
    -- 'NO_HISTORY' - no data available, using conservative estimate
    
    -- Assumed baseline values
    assumed_last_pm_date DATE,
    assumed_last_pm_mileage INTEGER,
    confidence_level confidence_level NOT NULL,  -- 'HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW', 'ESTIMATED'
    
    -- Inference source documentation
    inference_source TEXT,             -- Explanation of how assumption was derived
    inference_data JSONB,              -- Supporting data (e.g., higher PM dates)
    
    -- Review workflow
    requires_review BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    reviewed_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    review_notes TEXT,
    
    -- When applied
    applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    applied_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(train_id, pm_type_id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_train ON pm_baseline_assumptions(train_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_review ON pm_baseline_assumptions(requires_review) 
    WHERE requires_review = TRUE;
CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_confidence ON pm_baseline_assumptions(confidence_level)
    WHERE confidence_level IN ('LOW', 'ESTIMATED');

-- Example inference rules (implemented in application logic):
-- 1. If train commissioned 2020-01-01 and C1 interval is 6 months:
--    assumed_last_pm_date = 2020-01-01, confidence = 'LOW'
-- 2. If C4 completed 2024-06-15 and C4 satisfies C1/C2/C3:
--    For C1/C2/C3: assumed_last_pm_date = 2024-06-15, confidence = 'HIGH'
-- 3. If no data at all:
--    assumed_last_pm_date = commissioning_date, confidence = 'ESTIMATED', requires_review = TRUE
```

---

## 3.3 Mileage Tracking Tables

```sql
-- Meter events (odometer replacements, resets, swaps)
CREATE TABLE meter_events (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    event_date DATE NOT NULL,
    event_type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,  -- 'REPLACEMENT', 'RESET', 'CALIBRATION', 'SWAP'
    
    old_meter_serial VARCHAR(50),
    new_meter_serial VARCHAR(50),
    old_meter_reading INTEGER,
    new_meter_reading INTEGER,
    adjustment_km INTEGER,  -- Cumulative mileage adjustment to apply
    
    reason TEXT,
    approved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_meter_events_train ON meter_events(train_id, event_date DESC);

-- Daily mileage readings (TimescaleDB hypertable)
CREATE TABLE mileage_readings (
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    reading_date DATE NOT NULL,
    
    -- Raw and adjusted values
    raw_reading INTEGER NOT NULL,        -- Odometer value from CSV
    adjusted_reading INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- After meter event adjustments
    daily_km INTEGER,                    -- Calculated: today - yesterday
    
    -- Validation
    validation_status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'PENDING',  -- 'PENDING', 'VALID', 'ANOMALY', 'CORRECTED'
    anomaly_type VARCHAR(30),            -- 'NEGATIVE', 'SPIKE', 'MISSING', 'DUPLICATE'
    anomaly_notes TEXT,
    
    -- Source tracking
    source_file VARCHAR(255),
    meter_event_id UUID REFERENCES meter_events(id),
    
    ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    PRIMARY KEY (train_id, reading_date)
);

-- Convert to TimescaleDB hypertable
SELECT create_hypertable('mileage_readings', 'reading_date', 
    chunk_time_interval => INTERVAL '7 days',
    if_not_exists => TRUE
);

-- Compression policy (compress data older than 30 days)
SELECT add_compression_policy('mileage_readings', INTERVAL '30 days');

-- Retention policy (keep 13 years for contract + buffer)
SELECT add_retention_policy('mileage_readings', INTERVAL '13 years');

-- Indexes for common queries
CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_train_date ON mileage_readings (train_id, reading_date DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_validation ON mileage_readings (validation_status, reading_date DESC) 
    WHERE validation_status != 'VALID';

-- Continuous aggregate for dashboard queries
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW daily_fleet_mileage
WITH (timescaledb.continuous) AS
SELECT 
    time_bucket('1 day', reading_date) AS bucket,
    t.fleet_id,
    COUNT(*) as train_count,
    SUM(daily_km) as total_daily_km,
    AVG(daily_km) as avg_daily_km,
    MIN(daily_km) as min_daily_km,
    MAX(daily_km) as max_daily_km,
    STDDEV(daily_km) as stddev_daily_km
FROM mileage_readings mr
JOIN trains t ON mr.train_id = t.id
WHERE mr.validation_status = 'VALID'
GROUP BY bucket, t.fleet_id
WITH NO DATA;

SELECT add_continuous_aggregate_policy('daily_fleet_mileage',
    start_offset => INTERVAL '3 days',
    end_offset => INTERVAL '1 hour',
    schedule_interval => INTERVAL '1 hour'
);
```

## 3.4 Component Tracking Tables

```sql
-- Train components (bogies, motors, pantographs, etc.)
CREATE TABLE train_components (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    component_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,  -- 'BOGIE', 'TRACTION_MOTOR', 'PANTOGRAPH', 'BRAKE_UNIT'
    serial_number VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    manufacturer VARCHAR(100),
    model VARCHAR(100),
    
    -- Current installation
    current_train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
    current_position VARCHAR(50),  -- e.g., 'CAR_1_BOGIE_A', 'CAR_3_MOTOR_L'
    installed_date DATE,
    installed_mileage_km INTEGER,  -- Train mileage at installation
    
    -- Component lifecycle
    commissioning_date DATE,
    component_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Cumulative mileage on this component
    last_overhaul_date DATE,
    last_overhaul_mileage_km INTEGER,
    
    -- Status
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'IN_SERVICE',  -- 'IN_SERVICE', 'SPARE', 'IN_OVERHAUL', 'CONDEMNED'
    condition_score INTEGER,  -- 1-100 condition rating
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_components_train ON train_components(current_train_id) WHERE status = 'IN_SERVICE';
CREATE INDEX idx_components_type ON train_components(component_type, status);

-- Component installation history
CREATE TABLE component_installations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    component_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES train_components(id),
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    position VARCHAR(50),
    
    installed_date DATE NOT NULL,
    installed_train_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    installed_component_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    
    removed_date DATE,
    removed_train_mileage_km INTEGER,
    removed_component_mileage_km INTEGER,
    
    removal_reason VARCHAR(50),  -- 'SCHEDULED_OVERHAUL', 'FAILURE', 'SWAP', 'UPGRADE'
    work_order_id VARCHAR(50),   -- Link to Maximo
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_installations_component ON component_installations(component_id, installed_date DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_installations_train ON component_installations(train_id, installed_date DESC);

-- Component-based mileage adjustments
-- When a component is swapped, PM thresholds follow the component, not the train
CREATE TABLE component_pm_tracking (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    component_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES train_components(id),
    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
    
    last_pm_date DATE,
    last_pm_mileage_km INTEGER,
    next_pm_due_mileage_km INTEGER,
    next_pm_due_date DATE,
    
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(component_id, pm_type_id)
);
```

## 3.5 PM Management Tables

```sql
-- PM types (maintenance task definitions)
CREATE TABLE pm_types (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
    pm_code VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,      -- 'C1', 'C2', 'C3', 'C4', 'OVERHAUL'
    pm_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    version INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
    
    -- Interval thresholds (whichever comes first)
    interval_km INTEGER,               -- Mileage-based trigger
    interval_months INTEGER,           -- Time-based trigger (industry standard)
    
    -- PM Hierarchy: which lower-level PMs does this PM satisfy?
    -- When a C4 is completed, it satisfies C1/C2/C3 requirements
    -- This resets their mileage counters from the C4 completion point
    -- Example: C4 row has satisfies_codes = ['C1', 'C2', 'C3']
    satisfies_codes VARCHAR(20)[],
    
    -- Resource requirements
    estimated_hours DECIMAL(6,2) NOT NULL,
    track_type track_type NOT NULL,
    min_track_days INTEGER DEFAULT 1,  -- How many days track is occupied
    
    -- Compliance window (tolerance for scheduling)
    early_tolerance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 10.0,   -- Can do 10% early
    late_tolerance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 10.0,    -- Can do 10% late
    
    -- Certification requirements
    required_certifications VARCHAR(50)[],
    
    -- NEW v2.1: PM type versioning for interval changes over contract life
    effective_from DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    effective_to DATE,                              -- NULL = current version
    superseded_by UUID REFERENCES pm_types(id),    -- Link to newer version
    
    -- Canvas display
    display_color VARCHAR(7),
    display_priority INTEGER DEFAULT 50,
    
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(fleet_id, pm_code, version)
);

-- Index for finding current PM type version
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_types_current ON pm_types(fleet_id, pm_code, effective_from DESC)
    WHERE effective_to IS NULL AND active = TRUE;

-- Example PM hierarchy data:
-- INSERT INTO pm_types (fleet_id, pm_code, pm_name, interval_km, interval_months, satisfies_codes, ...)
-- KS Fleet:
--   C1: interval_km=12500, interval_months=6,  satisfies_codes=NULL
--   C2: interval_km=50000, interval_months=24, satisfies_codes=['C1']
--   C3: interval_km=100000, interval_months=48, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2']
--   C4: interval_km=200000, interval_months=96, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2','C3']
--   OH: interval_km=800000, interval_months=NULL, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2','C3','C4']

-- PM material requirements (Bill of Materials)
CREATE TABLE pm_material_requirements (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    part_description TEXT,
    
    quantity_per_pm INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    criticality material_criticality NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BLOCKING',
    -- BLOCKING = cannot start PM without part
    -- DEFERRABLE = can start, must have within X days
    -- CONSUMABLE = nice-to-have, can substitute
    
    lead_time_days INTEGER DEFAULT 14,
    substitute_parts VARCHAR(50)[],    -- Alternative part numbers
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(pm_type_id, part_number)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_pm_materials_type ON pm_material_requirements(pm_type_id);

-- PM schedule (planned and executed maintenance)
CREATE TABLE pm_schedule (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
    
    -- Scheduling
    target_date DATE NOT NULL,
    target_mileage_km INTEGER,
    status pm_status DEFAULT 'SCHEDULED',
    
    -- Compliance window (calculated from pm_types tolerances)
    constraint_start DATE NOT NULL,    -- Earliest allowed date
    constraint_end DATE NOT NULL,      -- Latest allowed date (statutory lock after this)
    
    -- Resource assignment
    assigned_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    assigned_track_id UUID REFERENCES tracks(id),
    assigned_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
    assigned_shift shift_type,
    
    -- Execution
    actual_start_date DATE,
    actual_end_date DATE,
    actual_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    completed_mileage_km INTEGER,
    
    -- Work order integration
    maximo_wo_id VARCHAR(50),
    
    -- Locking
    is_locked BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,   -- Cannot be moved by optimizer
    locked_reason VARCHAR(100),
    locked_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    locked_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    
    -- Scenario support (NULL = production schedule)
    scenario_id UUID REFERENCES scenarios(id),
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_train ON pm_schedule(train_id, target_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_date ON pm_schedule(target_date, status);
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_depot ON pm_schedule(assigned_depot_id, target_date) 
    WHERE status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS');
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_scenario ON pm_schedule(scenario_id) WHERE scenario_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_canvas ON pm_schedule(target_date, train_id)
    WHERE status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS') AND scenario_id IS NULL;
```

## 3.6 Material Management Tables

```sql
-- Inventory positions (snapshot from ERP/Maximo)
CREATE TABLE inventory_positions (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    part_description TEXT,
    
    -- Quantities
    quantity_on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    quantity_reserved INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Soft + hard reservations
    quantity_on_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    quantity_available INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (quantity_on_hand - quantity_reserved) STORED,
    
    -- Replenishment
    reorder_point INTEGER,
    reorder_quantity INTEGER,
    next_delivery_date DATE,
    next_delivery_quantity INTEGER,
    
    -- Cost
    unit_cost DECIMAL(12,2),
    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
    
    -- Sync tracking
    last_sync_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    source_system VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'MAXIMO',
    
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(depot_id, part_number)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_depot ON inventory_positions(depot_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_part ON inventory_positions(part_number);
CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_low_stock ON inventory_positions(depot_id, part_number) 
    WHERE quantity_available <= reorder_point;

-- Material reservations (link inventory to PM schedule)
CREATE TABLE material_reservations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    pm_schedule_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_schedule(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    inventory_position_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES inventory_positions(id),
    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    
    quantity_reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
    reservation_type reservation_type DEFAULT 'SOFT',
    -- SOFT = tentative, can be released if PM moves
    -- HARD = committed, parts allocated
    
    reserved_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    reserved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- SOFT reservations auto-expire
    
    -- Fulfillment
    fulfilled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    fulfilled_quantity INTEGER,
    
    UNIQUE(pm_schedule_id, part_number)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_reservations_pm ON material_reservations(pm_schedule_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_reservations_inventory ON material_reservations(inventory_position_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_reservations_expiring ON material_reservations(expires_at) 
    WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT' AND fulfilled_at IS NULL;
```

## 3.7 Man-hour Planning Tables

```sql
-- Man-hour capacity (resource availability by depot)
CREATE TABLE manhour_capacity (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
    effective_to DATE,
    
    -- Base capacity per shift type
    day_shift_hours DECIMAL(8,2) NOT NULL,
    night_shift_hours DECIMAL(8,2) DEFAULT 0,
    weekend_hours DECIMAL(8,2) DEFAULT 0,
    
    -- Staff counts
    technicians_day INTEGER NOT NULL,
    technicians_night INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    specialists INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    
    -- Constraints
    max_overtime_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 20.0,
    min_utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 70.0,
    target_utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 85.0,
    
    notes TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(depot_id, effective_from)
);

-- Man-hour projections (generated by leveling engine)
CREATE TABLE manhour_projections (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    projection_run_id UUID NOT NULL,
    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
    
    period_start DATE NOT NULL,
    period_end DATE NOT NULL,
    granularity VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,  -- 'WEEKLY', 'MONTHLY'
    
    -- Required hours breakdown
    required_pm_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    required_corrective_hours DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
    required_overhaul_hours DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
    required_total_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    
    -- Available hours
    available_regular_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    available_overtime_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    available_total_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    
    -- Computed metrics
    variance_hours DECIMAL(10,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (available_total_hours - required_total_hours) STORED,
    utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (CASE WHEN available_total_hours > 0 
              THEN (required_total_hours / available_total_hours * 100)
              ELSE 0 END) STORED,
    is_overloaded BOOLEAN GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (required_total_hours > available_total_hours) STORED,
    is_underutilized BOOLEAN GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (required_total_hours < available_total_hours * 0.7) STORED,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_proj_depot ON manhour_projections(depot_id, period_start);
CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_proj_run ON manhour_projections(projection_run_id);

-- Man-hour adjustments (proposed changes to level workload)
CREATE TABLE manhour_adjustments (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    projection_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES manhour_projections(id),
    
    adjustment_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    -- 'ADD_OVERTIME', 'DEFER_PM', 'ADVANCE_PM', 'CROSS_DEPOT', 'ADD_CONTRACTOR'
    
    source_period_start DATE,
    source_period_end DATE,
    target_period_start DATE,
    target_period_end DATE,
    hours_adjusted DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    
    affected_pm_ids UUID[] DEFAULT '{}',
    affected_train_ids UUID[] DEFAULT '{}',
    
    -- Impact assessment
    cost_impact DECIMAL(12,2),
    risk_impact VARCHAR(20),  -- 'NONE', 'LOW', 'MEDIUM', 'HIGH'
    risk_description TEXT,
    
    -- Workflow
    status adjustment_status DEFAULT 'PROPOSED',
    proposed_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    proposed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    approved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    rejection_reason TEXT,
    
    -- Explanation
    reason TEXT NOT NULL,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_adj_projection ON manhour_adjustments(projection_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_adj_status ON manhour_adjustments(status) WHERE status = 'PROPOSED';
```

## 3.8 Baseline & Drift Tables

```sql
-- Plan baselines (immutable snapshots)
CREATE TABLE plan_baselines (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    version VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,  -- e.g., 'v3.2'
    
    baseline_date DATE NOT NULL,   -- "As of" date
    horizon_end DATE NOT NULL,     -- How far into future
    
    -- Workflow
    status baseline_status DEFAULT 'DRAFT',
    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    published_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    published_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    
    superseded_by UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
    superseded_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    
    -- Metrics at baseline time
    fleet_count INTEGER,
    total_pm_scheduled INTEGER,
    total_manhours_projected DECIMAL(12,2)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_baselines_status ON plan_baselines(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_baselines_date ON plan_baselines(baseline_date DESC);

-- Baseline PM schedule snapshot
CREATE TABLE baseline_pm_schedule (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    baseline_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_baselines(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
    projected_date DATE NOT NULL,
    projected_mileage_km INTEGER,
    
    assigned_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    assigned_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
    
    UNIQUE(baseline_id, train_id, pm_type_id, projected_date)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_baseline_pm_baseline ON baseline_pm_schedule(baseline_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_baseline_pm_train ON baseline_pm_schedule(train_id, projected_date);

-- Baseline mileage projections
CREATE TABLE baseline_mileage_projection (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    baseline_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_baselines(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    projection_date DATE NOT NULL,
    projected_cumulative_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    daily_rate_km INTEGER,  -- Assumed daily accrual rate
    
    UNIQUE(baseline_id, train_id, projection_date)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_baseline_mileage ON baseline_mileage_projection(baseline_id, train_id);

-- Drift analysis results (generated by drift monitor)
CREATE TABLE drift_analysis (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    baseline_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
    analysis_date DATE NOT NULL,
    
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    
    -- Mileage drift
    baseline_projected_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    actual_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    mileage_drift_km INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS (actual_km - baseline_projected_km) STORED,
    mileage_drift_pct DECIMAL(5,2),
    
    -- Schedule drift (days)
    avg_pm_drift_days DECIMAL(5,1),
    max_pm_drift_days INTEGER,
    
    -- Threshold breach
    requires_replan BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    replan_reason TEXT,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(baseline_id, train_id, analysis_date)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_drift_baseline ON drift_analysis(baseline_id, analysis_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_drift_replan ON drift_analysis(requires_replan, analysis_date) 
    WHERE requires_replan = TRUE;
```

## 3.9 Scenario Management Tables

```sql
-- Scenarios (what-if analysis containers)
CREATE TABLE scenarios (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    
    -- Fork point
    forked_from_baseline_id UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
    forked_from_scenario_id UUID REFERENCES scenarios(id),
    forked_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    -- Metadata
    status scenario_status DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    -- Expiration (scenarios auto-expire after 30 days of inactivity)
    last_modified_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT (NOW() + INTERVAL '30 days'),
    
    -- Promotion
    promoted_to_baseline_id UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
    promoted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    promoted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_scenarios_status ON scenarios(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_scenarios_user ON scenarios(created_by, status);
CREATE INDEX idx_scenarios_expiring ON scenarios(expires_at) WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';

-- Scenario deltas (event-sourced changes)
CREATE TABLE scenario_deltas (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    scenario_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES scenarios(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    sequence_num INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Order of application
    
    delta_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    -- 'PM_MOVE', 'PM_ADD', 'PM_REMOVE', 'CAPACITY_CHANGE', 'TRAIN_STATUS_CHANGE'
    
    -- Reference to affected entity
    entity_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,  -- 'pm_schedule', 'manhour_capacity', 'trains'
    entity_id UUID NOT NULL,
    
    -- Change details (JSON)
    old_values JSONB,
    new_values JSONB,
    
    -- Explanation
    change_reason TEXT,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    
    UNIQUE(scenario_id, sequence_num)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_scenario_deltas_scenario ON scenario_deltas(scenario_id, sequence_num);

-- Scenario comparison results (materialized for performance)
CREATE TABLE scenario_comparisons (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    scenario_a_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES scenarios(id),
    scenario_b_id UUID REFERENCES scenarios(id),  -- NULL = compare to baseline
    baseline_id UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
    
    comparison_date DATE NOT NULL,
    
    -- Summary metrics
    pm_count_delta INTEGER,
    total_hours_delta DECIMAL(10,2),
    total_cost_delta DECIMAL(12,2),
    overloaded_weeks_delta INTEGER,
    
    -- Detailed differences (JSON)
    pm_differences JSONB,
    manhour_differences JSONB,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

## 3.10 Costing Tables

```sql
-- Labor rates
CREATE TABLE labor_rates (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),  -- NULL = global default
    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
    effective_to DATE,
    
    -- Rate types
    regular_hourly_rate DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    overtime_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 1.5,
    weekend_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 2.0,
    holiday_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 2.5,
    
    -- Shop rate (fully loaded)
    shop_rate_hourly DECIMAL(10,2),  -- Includes overhead, benefits
    
    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(depot_id, effective_from)
);

-- Material cost history
CREATE TABLE material_costs (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
    effective_to DATE,
    
    unit_cost DECIMAL(12,2) NOT NULL,
    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
    supplier VARCHAR(100),
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(part_number, effective_from)
);

-- Cost baselines (for variance analysis)
CREATE TABLE cost_baselines (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    fiscal_year INTEGER NOT NULL,
    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    
    budget_labor_hours DECIMAL(12,2),
    budget_labor_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
    budget_material_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
    budget_total DECIMAL(14,2),
    
    -- Actuals (updated monthly)
    actual_labor_hours DECIMAL(12,2),
    actual_labor_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
    actual_material_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
    actual_total DECIMAL(14,2),
    
    variance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (CASE WHEN budget_total > 0 
              THEN ((actual_total - budget_total) / budget_total * 100)
              ELSE 0 END) STORED,
    
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(fiscal_year, depot_id)
);
```

## 3.11 Audit & History Tables

```sql
-- PM schedule change history (immutable audit trail)
CREATE TABLE pm_schedule_history (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    pm_schedule_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_schedule(id),
    
    change_type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
    -- 'CREATED', 'DATE_CHANGED', 'STATUS_CHANGED', 'TRACK_ASSIGNED', 
    -- 'LOCKED', 'UNLOCKED', 'COMPLETED', 'CANCELLED', 'DEFERRED'
    
    -- Previous values
    old_target_date DATE,
    old_status pm_status,
    old_assigned_track_id UUID,
    old_assigned_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
    
    -- New values
    new_target_date DATE,
    new_status pm_status,
    new_assigned_track_id UUID,
    new_assigned_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
    
    -- Attribution
    changed_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    change_reason TEXT,
    change_source VARCHAR(30) DEFAULT 'USER',  -- 'USER', 'OPTIMIZER', 'SYSTEM', 'IMPORT'
    
    -- Optimizer context (if change was suggested by optimizer)
    optimizer_request_id UUID,
    optimizer_explanation JSONB
);

CREATE INDEX idx_pm_history_schedule ON pm_schedule_history(pm_schedule_id, changed_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_history_date ON pm_schedule_history(changed_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_history_user ON pm_schedule_history(changed_by, changed_at DESC);

-- General audit log
CREATE TABLE audit_log (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    
    -- What
    entity_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    entity_id UUID NOT NULL,
    action VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,  -- 'CREATE', 'UPDATE', 'DELETE', 'APPROVE', 'REJECT', 'PUBLISH'
    
    -- Who
    user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    user_email VARCHAR(255),
    user_ip_address INET,
    
    -- When
    occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    -- Details
    old_values JSONB,
    new_values JSONB,
    metadata JSONB,  -- Additional context
    
    -- Compliance
    compliance_flags VARCHAR(50)[]  -- e.g., ['SAFETY_CRITICAL', 'FINANCIAL']
);

CREATE INDEX idx_audit_entity ON audit_log(entity_type, entity_id, occurred_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_user ON audit_log(user_id, occurred_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_date ON audit_log(occurred_at DESC);
```

---

## 3.12 Users & Authentication Tables

```sql
-- Users
CREATE TABLE users (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    
    first_name VARCHAR(100),
    last_name VARCHAR(100),
    display_name VARCHAR(200),
    
    -- Assignment
    primary_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    role_ids UUID[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
    
    -- Certifications (for technician constraint matching)
    certifications VARCHAR(50)[],
    
    -- Status
    is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    last_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    failed_login_attempts INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    locked_until TIMESTAMPTZ,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email);
CREATE INDEX idx_users_depot ON users(primary_depot_id) WHERE is_active = TRUE;

-- Roles
CREATE TABLE roles (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    description TEXT,
    permissions JSONB NOT NULL,  -- e.g., {"pm_schedule": ["read", "write"], "baseline": ["read"]}
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Sessions (Redis-backed, but schema for reference)
CREATE TABLE sessions (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    token_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    last_activity_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    ip_address INET,
    user_agent TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_user ON sessions(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_expiry ON sessions(expires_at);
```

---

## 3.13 Canvas-Optimized Views

```sql
-- Strategic zoom level (yearly aggregates)
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_strategic AS
SELECT 
    t.id as train_id,
    t.train_number,
    t.fleet_id,
    date_trunc('year', ps.target_date) as year,
    COUNT(*) as pm_count,
    SUM(pt.estimated_hours) as total_hours,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ps.status = 'COMPLETED') as completed_count,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ps.is_locked) as locked_count,
    SUM(pt.estimated_hours) FILTER (WHERE ps.status = 'SCHEDULED') as pending_hours
FROM trains t
LEFT JOIN pm_schedule ps ON t.id = ps.train_id AND ps.scenario_id IS NULL
LEFT JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
WHERE t.active = TRUE
GROUP BY t.id, t.train_number, t.fleet_id, date_trunc('year', ps.target_date);

CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_strategic_train ON v_canvas_strategic(train_id, year);

-- Tactical zoom level (weekly aggregates with PM details)
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_tactical AS
SELECT 
    t.id as train_id,
    t.train_number,
    date_trunc('week', ps.target_date) as week_start,
    ps.id as pm_id,
    ps.target_date,
    ps.status,
    ps.constraint_start,
    ps.constraint_end,
    ps.is_locked,
    pt.pm_code,
    pt.estimated_hours,
    pt.display_color,
    tr.track_number,
    d.code as depot_code
FROM trains t
JOIN pm_schedule ps ON t.id = ps.train_id AND ps.scenario_id IS NULL
JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
LEFT JOIN tracks tr ON ps.assigned_track_id = tr.id
LEFT JOIN depots d ON ps.assigned_depot_id = d.id
WHERE t.active = TRUE
  AND ps.status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS');

CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_tactical_train ON v_canvas_tactical(train_id, week_start);
CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_tactical_date ON v_canvas_tactical(target_date);

-- Man-hour canvas overlay
CREATE VIEW v_manhour_canvas AS
SELECT 
    mp.depot_id,
    d.code as depot_code,
    mp.period_start,
    mp.period_end,
    mp.required_total_hours,
    mp.available_total_hours,
    mp.variance_hours,
    mp.utilization_pct,
    mp.is_overloaded,
    mp.is_underutilized,
    COALESCE(adj.adjustment_count, 0) as pending_adjustments,
    COALESCE(adj.total_adjustment_hours, 0) as proposed_adjustment_hours
FROM manhour_projections mp
JOIN depots d ON mp.depot_id = d.id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        projection_id,
        COUNT(*) as adjustment_count,
        SUM(hours_adjusted) as total_adjustment_hours
    FROM manhour_adjustments
    WHERE status = 'PROPOSED'
    GROUP BY projection_id
) adj ON mp.id = adj.projection_id
WHERE mp.projection_run_id = (
    SELECT projection_run_id 
    FROM manhour_projections 
    ORDER BY created_at DESC 
    LIMIT 1
);

-- Drift overlay (comparison to baseline)
CREATE VIEW v_drift_canvas AS
SELECT 
    da.train_id,
    t.train_number,
    da.baseline_id,
    pb.name as baseline_name,
    da.analysis_date,
    da.mileage_drift_km,
    da.mileage_drift_pct,
    da.avg_pm_drift_days,
    da.requires_replan,
    da.replan_reason,
    -- Color coding for canvas
    CASE 
        WHEN da.requires_replan THEN 'RED'
        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 3 THEN 'YELLOW'
        ELSE 'GREEN'
    END as drift_severity
FROM drift_analysis da
JOIN trains t ON da.train_id = t.id
JOIN plan_baselines pb ON da.baseline_id = pb.id
WHERE pb.status = 'PUBLISHED'
  AND da.analysis_date = (
      SELECT MAX(analysis_date) 
      FROM drift_analysis 
      WHERE baseline_id = da.baseline_id
  );

-- Refresh materialized views (scheduled job)
-- REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
-- REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
```

---

## 3.14 Database Maintenance

```sql
-- Automatic refresh of materialized views
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION refresh_canvas_views()
RETURNS void AS $$
BEGIN
    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Schedule refresh every 15 minutes (use pg_cron or external scheduler)
-- SELECT cron.schedule('*/15 * * * *', 'SELECT refresh_canvas_views()');

-- Cleanup expired scenarios
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION cleanup_expired_scenarios()
RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
DECLARE
    deleted_count INTEGER;
BEGIN
    UPDATE scenarios 
    SET status = 'EXPIRED'
    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' 
      AND expires_at < NOW();
    
    GET DIAGNOSTICS deleted_count = ROW_COUNT;
    RETURN deleted_count;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Release expired soft reservations
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION release_expired_reservations()
RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
DECLARE
    released_count INTEGER;
BEGIN
    -- Update inventory positions
    UPDATE inventory_positions ip
    SET quantity_reserved = quantity_reserved - expired.total_qty
    FROM (
        SELECT inventory_position_id, SUM(quantity_reserved) as total_qty
        FROM material_reservations
        WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
          AND expires_at < NOW()
          AND fulfilled_at IS NULL
        GROUP BY inventory_position_id
    ) expired
    WHERE ip.id = expired.inventory_position_id;
    
    -- Delete expired reservations
    DELETE FROM material_reservations
    WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
      AND expires_at < NOW()
      AND fulfilled_at IS NULL;
    
    GET DIAGNOSTICS released_count = ROW_COUNT;
    RETURN released_count;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Update train target mileage for fleet balancing
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_fleet_mileage_targets()
RETURNS void AS $$
BEGIN
    UPDATE trains t
    SET target_mileage_km = fleet_avg.avg_mileage
    FROM (
        SELECT fleet_id, AVG(current_mileage_km)::INTEGER as avg_mileage
        FROM trains
        WHERE active = TRUE
        GROUP BY fleet_id
    ) fleet_avg
    WHERE t.fleet_id = fleet_avg.fleet_id
      AND t.active = TRUE;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
```

file.content:
## 6.9 Accessibility Requirements

### 6.9.1 WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance

| Requirement | Implementation |
|-------------|----------------|
| Keyboard navigation | Full canvas navigation via arrow keys, Tab for controls |
| Screen reader | ARIA labels for all interactive elements |
| Color contrast | 4.5:1 minimum for text, 3:1 for large text |
| Focus indicators | Visible focus ring on all interactive elements |
| Alternative text | All icons have text alternatives |
| Reduced motion | Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` setting |

### 6.9.2 Keyboard Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` | Navigate between panels |
| `Arrow keys` | Pan canvas |
| `+` / `-` | Zoom in/out |
| `Home` | Go to today |
| `1-5` | Switch zoom levels |
| `Escape` | Cancel drag / Close dialog |
| `Enter` | Activate selected element |
| `Space` | Toggle selection |
| `?` | Show keyboard shortcuts |

---

## 6.10 Responsive Behavior

### 6.10.1 Breakpoints

| Breakpoint | Width | Layout Changes |
|------------|-------|----------------|
| Desktop XL | ГўвҖ°ВҘ1920px | Full layout with all panels |
| Desktop | ГўвҖ°ВҘ1440px | Standard layout |
| Desktop SM | ГўвҖ°ВҘ1280px | Collapsible side panels |
| Tablet | ГўвҖ°ВҘ1024px | Stacked panels, simplified canvas |
| Mobile | <1024px | Not supported (show message) |

## 6.11 Performance Requirements

| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| Initial load | <3s | Time to first meaningful paint |
| Canvas render | 60 FPS | Frame rate during pan/zoom |
| Data points | 500,000+ | Maximum renderable PM blocks |
| Interaction response | <100ms | Time from click to visual feedback |
| API response display | <500ms | Time from API response to UI update |
| Memory usage | <1GB | Maximum heap size |

### 6.11.1 Optimization Strategies

```typescript
const PERFORMANCE_OPTIMIZATIONS = {
  // Data loading
  virtualScrolling: true,           // Only render visible train rows
  tileBasedLoading: true,           // Load canvas data in tiles
  progressiveLoading: true,         // Load coarse data first, refine
  
  // Rendering
  webWorkerCalculations: true,      // Offload heavy math to workers
  gpuAcceleration: true,            // Use WebGL via deck.gl
  memoizedFiltering: true,          // Cache filtered datasets
  levelOfDetail: true,              // Reduce detail at low zoom
  
  // Caching
  apiResponseCache: '5m',           // Cache API responses
  canvasDataCache: '1m',            // Cache canvas tile data
  userPreferencesCache: 'session',  // Cache user settings
};
```
